source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, points A, B, and C are on the circle with center O. If $\angle C=20^\circ$ and $\angle B=30^\circ$, what is the measure of $\angle A$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Let $\because$ $\angle C=20^\circ$,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle O=2\angle C=40^\circ$,\\
$\because$ $\angle O+\angle A=\angle B+\angle C$,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle A=30^\circ+20^\circ-40^\circ=\boxed{10^\circ}$

geometry setup refers to positions/angles of A, B, C, and center O on a circle, which are not fully specified in text alone
asset_registry_record shows an image exists and registry_passed=true

the task requires integrating given angle values with the circle diagram structure
verifiability: the target is a single numeric angle measure, and answer_text gives a concrete final result of 10 degrees
answer_text contains an explicit derivation that can be checked step by step against geometry rules

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the sample retains recoverable core semantics
the problem is highly image-dependent, but the image is very small (84x93) with a readability risk flag
